_help: The only supporters file you edit by hand. It controls how Buy Me a Coffee names are turned into the privacy-safe names shown on the site. Matching is case-insensitive on the Buy Me a Coffee 'payer name'.
_privacyMode_options: How to anonymize names that have no explicit alias. 'first' = first name only (Marcello Mastroeni -> Marcello). 'initial' = first name + surname initial (Marcello M.). 'mask' = first/last letter with bullets (Marcello -> M••••••o). 'full' = show the name as-is (NOT anonymous).
_topCount_note: How many of the biggest supporters get the highlighted podium (1st/2nd/3rd). 3 = a full gold/silver/bronze podium.
_aliases_note: Map a real Buy Me a Coffee name to a custom display alias, e.g. { "Marcello Mastroeni": "Marci" }. Aliases bypass masking.
_hide_note: Real names to exclude from the wall entirely (e.g. on request), e.g. [ "Someone Private" ].
_forceAnonymous_note: Real names to show as 'Anonymous supporter' instead of any masked form.
_manualSupporters_note: Supporters to add by hand for a channel the APIs don't cover (e.g. a GitHub Sponsor before GH_SPONSORS_TOKEN is set). Shown EXACTLY as typed (no masking) — only list a name the person is happy to show publicly. Each item is a string, or { "name": "...", "tier": "member", "coffees": N }. `coffees` is a FALLBACK weight (~$5 each) used only until an API ranks this same person — once GitHub Sponsors / BMC returns them, the real amount wins and the manual coffees are ignored (no double-count), so the podium stays honest and a bigger donor auto-overtakes. `tier:"member"` highlights them. Avoid `top:true` (a hard pin to #1) unless you really want them frozen at the top regardless of amount.
